message:
fix(abi): prevent out-of-bounds read in string functions when scan_c_string hits bound limit

In 8 string ABI functions (strspn, strcspn, strpbrk, strcasestr, strlcpy,
strlcat, strcoll, strxfrm), the code previously discarded the `terminated`
boolean returned by `scan_c_string()` and unconditionally added +1 to the
slice length assuming a null terminator was always found. When scan_c_string
reached the bound limit before finding a null terminator, this caused an
out-of-bounds read by indexing one byte past the valid range.

Now each function checks the `terminated` flag and only includes the +1 for
the null terminator when one was actually found. Also fixes `psignal` to
skip prepending the ": " separator when the user-provided prefix is an
empty string (points to "\0").

Includes rustfmt reformatting of test assertions in the workload API wave
plan test (no logic changes in test file).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
